item.sentence: Although accidental pollution is spectacular, it is still recognised that <operational> pollution remains the bigger threat.
Therefore a new procedure, called the <load-on-top> system, was introduced in 1969.
The <washings> resulting from tank cleaning are pumped into a special tank.
During the voyage to the <loading terminal> the oil and water separate.
The enormous growth in the transport of oil by <shipping>, but also the increasing amount of chemicals carried at sea, made it clear that the OILPOL Convention was no longer adequate.
The enormous growth in the transport of oil by shipping, but also the increasing amount of <chemicals> carried at sea, made it clear that the OILPOL Convention was no longer adequate.
The Sub-Committee on Oil Pollution became the Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC) and was given the same <standing> as the Maritime Safety Committee (MSC).
The Protocol of the Conference on Tanker Safety and Pollution Prevention of 1978 expanded the requirements for <segregated ballast> tanks to new crude oil tankers.
The Protocol of the Conference on Tanker Safety and Pollution Prevention of 1978 expanded the requirements for segregated ballast tanks to new <crude oil> tankers.
<Crude oil washing> is the cleaning of cargo tanks with high pressure jets of crude oil.
Crude oil washing is the cleaning of cargo tanks with <high pressure> jets of crude oil.
<Inert gases>, low in oxygen and as a consequence incombustible, are used to replace flammable gases in tanks.
Inert gases, low in oxygen and as a consequence incombustible, are used to replace <flammable> gases in tanks.
OPA 90 made it mandatory for all tankers <calling at> ports in the United States to have double hulls.
The IMO carried out a study into the comparative performances of the double hull and the <mid-deck> tanker designs.
The proposals were presented to the MEPC to consider an accelerated ‘single-hull oil tanker <phase-out>’ schedule.
The Prestige disaster also led to an extended application of the <Condition Assessment Scheme> for tankers.
The CAS requirements oblige shipowners to have documentary and <survey> procedures properly carried out and completed.
